I've been studying this concept for a while now, and I'm particularly interested in how it plays out in high-stress professions like law enforcement. I've been collecting data and conducting interviews with police officers, and I'm starting to see some really interesting patterns emerge. For example, I've found that officers who are required to suppress their emotions on the job (e.g. when dealing with traumatic situations) tend to experience higher levels of burnout and lower job satisfaction. I'm wondering if anyone else has looked into this topic, or if you have any insights to share. I'm also curious about potential interventions that could help mitigate the negative effects of emotional labor on officer well-being. Any thoughts or advice would be greatly appreciated!
